pattern: Grouping by the key that must differ and keeping only each group's best value reduces a distinct-triplet search to picking the three largest group bests.
hints: Two indices sharing the same x value can never both be chosen, so within an x group only the largest y could ever matter.
Collapse each distinct x to the maximum y seen for it.
If at least three distinct x values exist, sum the three largest collapsed values; otherwise return -1.

edge_inputs.description: three distinct x exist with the example values
only two distinct x means no valid triplet
exactly three indices all distinct x
exactly three but a repeated x blocks it
best y per group, not best three y overall
many groups, pick top three group maxima
all x distinct, take top three y

stress.description: random x with collisions and random y
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 120))
    x = [rng.randrange(1, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    y = [rng.randrange(1, 10**6 + 1) for _ in range(n)]
    return [x, y]
